items.title: Bitdeer AI Wins “AI Cloud Platform of the Year” in 2026 AI Breakthrough Awards, Recognized as a Global Leader in AI Cloud Infrastructure - markets.businessinsider.com
items.summary:
PRESS RELEASE GlobeNewswire

Jun. 25, 2026, 10:00 PM

SINGAPORE, June 26, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- B
items.content:
PRESS RELEASE GlobeNewswire

Jun. 25, 2026, 10:00 PM

SINGAPORE, June 26, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bitdeer AI, part of Bitdeer Technologies Group (NASDAQ: BTDR) and a preferred NVIDIA Cloud Partner, today announced it has been the winner of “AI Cloud Platform of the Year” in 2026 AI Breakthrough Awards. The award is presented by AI Breakthrough, a leading market intelligence organization that recognizes the top companies, technologies and products in the global AI market today. The prestigious accolade recognizes Bitdeer AI’s architectural breakthrough in delivering a fully integrated, full-stack AI cloud environment optimized for enterprise-scale generative AI and production workloads. [...] My Markets Watchlist

Markets   News

# Bitdeer AI Wins “AI Cloud Platform of the Year” in 2026 AI Breakthrough Awards, Recognized as a Global Leader in AI Cloud Infrastructure

PRESS RELEASE GlobeNewswire

Jun. 25, 2026, 10:00 PM [...] "Bitdeer AI has built one of the most complete and capable full-stack AI cloud platforms available today, combining high-performance GPU infrastructure with an enterprise-ready AI development environment," said Steve Johansson, Managing Director, AI Breakthrough.

“We are honored to receive this recognition from AI Breakthrough for the second consecutive year,” said Retainna Lin, VP of Bitdeer AI Cloud. “The winners of the next era of AI will be determined by execution speed and infrastructure reliability. At Bitdeer AI, we have engineered a full-stack cloud platform that compresses the journey from raw GPU compute to live production. We are building the foundational operating layer for global AI deployment.”

items.title: China's Zhipu is closing in on top U.S. AI models with Anthropic and OpenAI held back - CNBC
items.summary: The Chinese AI startup's new open source model has blown past every other open release and now sits
items.content:
The Chinese AI startup's new open source model has blown past every other open release and now sits within a percentage point of Anthropic's Opus 4.8 on one closely watched agentic benchmark, at roughly a fifth of the cost.

Developers are piling in, with OpenRouter token traffic climbing faster than it did after DeepSeek's V4 launch in April. And unlike DeepSeek, which the market eventually dismissed as a one-off chatbot shock, GLM 5.2 is strong at agentic work like planning, coding, testing, and looping, the kind of work that enterprises are racing to automate.

Companies hit by unexpectedly high AI spend on tokens, the measure of the data processed and generated by AI models, are now asking how to get the most for their money. [...] GLM 5.2 is free to download, fine-tune, and run on an enterprise's own servers, putting pricing pressure on frontier labs at the same time that access looks shaky. Anthropic had to pull its Fable Mythos-class model after an order by the Trump administration, and OpenAI announced Friday that it is limiting its GPT 5.6 models because of a government request.

The federal oversight has made a model that no one can revoke increasingly look like the safer bet.

items.title: China’s DeepSeek whose low-cost AI model shocked tech industry plans to double its workforce - The Times of India
items.summary: Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek has announced plans to double the size of its workf
items.content: Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek has announced plans to double the size of its workforce following a massive, multi-billion-dollar fundraising round, a report has said. The expansion comes as the tech firm looks to heavily compete against US generative AI heavyweights like OpenAI and Anthropic. In a statement released on WeChat (via The Wall Street Journal), the Hangzhou-based company revealed it is immediately opening applications for 27 different types of technical and corporate roles.DeepSeek is recruiting development engineers, data engineers, AI product managers and operations staff, alongside scaling up its core corporate departments including human resources, legal, and finance. The AI company also noted that all of these positions are open to student

items.title: Two Decades of Precision: Lingyi iTech Marks 20th Anniversary with Landmark Hong Kong Listing (1688.HK) to Anchor Its Global AI Leadership - The Manila Times
items.summary:
Advertisement

AI Infrastructure: Strengthening Server Architecture

The massive computational requi
items.content:
Advertisement

AI Infrastructure: Strengthening Server Architecture

The massive computational requirements of large language models have placed unprecedented stress on data center hardware layers. Lingyi iTech has successfully positioned itself as a critical hardware provider for the high-density AI server market, through its integrated "Thermal Management + Power Supply" solutions.

Readore and the Liquid Cooling Imperative

A critical element of Lingyi iTech's absolute competitive advantage in the AI infrastructure space is its majority-owned subsidiary, Dongguan Readore Technology Co., Ltd.. As AI clusters scale up to advanced graphics processing architectures, traditional air cooling hits its physical limits, making advanced liquid cooling mandatory for next-generation server racks.

items.title: China's Zhipu is closing in on top U.S. AI models with Anthropic and OpenAI held back - CNBC
items.summary: The Chinese AI startup's new open source model has blown past every other open release and now sits
items.content:
The Chinese AI startup's new open source model has blown past every other open release and now sits within a percentage point of Anthropic's Opus 4.8 on one closely watched agentic benchmark, at roughly a fifth of the cost.

Developers are piling in, with OpenRouter token traffic climbing faster than it did after DeepSeek's V4 launch in April. And unlike DeepSeek, which the market eventually dismissed as a one-off chatbot shock, GLM 5.2 is strong at agentic work like planning, coding, testing, and looping, the kind of work that enterprises are racing to automate.

Companies hit by unexpectedly high AI spend on tokens, the measure of the data processed and generated by AI models, are now asking how to get the most for their money. [...] The most important metric is becoming intelligence per dollar, making Zhipu's cheap-but-good-enough model an attractive answer.

"I've been consistently surprised by how quickly the open source has caught up," Gabe Pereyra, co-founder of Harvey, told CNBC. "GLM 5.2, you're seeing the first model where it's really competitive with some of these closed-source frontier models."

But the real story underneath the price tag is the open source AI moment. [...] Jasmine Wu@jaswu\_

WATCH LIVE

Key Points

 Zhipu's GLM 5.2 lands within a percentage point of Anthropic's Opus 4.8 on a key agentic benchmark at roughly a fifth of the cost.
 As frontier token spend strains budgets, enterprises are asking how to get the most for their money, making intelligence per dollar the metric that matters.
 With government limits restricting the rollout of Anthropic's Fable and OpenAI's GPT-5.6, a free model makes open source an attractive alternative.

In this article

 KNWAY

watch now

VIDEO52:1952:19

How Z.AI is closing in on America's AI frontier

Tech

Zhipu's GLM 5.2 artificial intelligence model landed last week with the kind of Silicon Valley buzz that followed DeepSeek's launch last year.

All custom code used for the evaluation pipeline, adversarial stress tests, rubric-based analyses, statistical analyses and figure generation is available under the MIT License in the accompanying repository, with the exact version used to generate the reported results archived at Zenodo: . The repository includes inference and evaluation pipelines, prompt specifications, perturbation generators, scoring scripts, statistical notebooks and figure generation scripts. Full replication requires access to the relevant commercial model APIs and datasets.

## References

1. Singhal, K. et al. Large language models encode clinical knowledge. Nature 620, 172–180 (2023).

items.title: Limitless Labs raises $20 million to expand AI platform for CNC programming and precision manufacturing - Robotics & Automation News
items.summary:
Others

Other uncategorized cookies are those that are being analyzed and have not been classified i
items.content:
Others

Other uncategorized cookies are those that are being analyzed and have not been classified into a category as yet.

SAVE & ACCEPT [...] Unlike general-purpose AI models trained on text or software code, Limitless Labs says its Physical AI Foundation Model has been trained on manufacturing-specific data, including CAD geometry, machining physics, and the operational characteristics of CNC machines.

The company says the technology is intended to help manufacturers address growing shortages of experienced CNC programmers while preserving specialist knowledge that often resides with a relatively small number of highly skilled machinists.

David Priev, co-founder and CEO of Limitless Labs, says: “The manufacturing world doesn’t just need more automation, it needs a better way to capture and scale the expertise that still lives inside the heads of a relatively small number of experienced machinists. [...] Manage consent

Out of these, the cookies that are categorized as necessary are stored on your browser as they are essential for the working of basic functionalities of the website. We also use third-party cookies that help us analyze and understand how you use this website. These cookies will be stored in your browser only with your consent. You also have the option to opt-out of these cookies. But opting out of some of these cookies may affect your browsing experience.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic face new AI reality as companies shift from tokenmaxxing to efficiency - CNBC
items.summary: In a June essay, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said the industry needs to avoid concentrating power in
items.content:
In a June essay, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said the industry needs to avoid concentrating power in a handful of large providers.

"The last thing any of us want is a world where every company across every sector is ceding value to a few models that eat everything they see," Nadella wrote. "If all the value is accrued by only a few models, the political economy will simply not tolerate it."

CEO Satya Nadella speaks during Microsoft Build 2026.

Courtesy: Microsoft

Amazon and Google are also ramping up their investments in models for business users. [...] As for going public, neither of the big model companies have provided an exact timeframe for their prospective debuts. The New York Times reported on Thursday, citing people involved in the deliberations, that OpenAI is leaning toward holding off until next year.

Pressure to go public may revolve around the need for capital. With Anthropic and OpenAI increasingly competing against their biggest financial backers, the IPO market may be the best avenue for new money, especially as their capital needs have become too great for most venture and private equity firms.

items.title: China's Zhipu is closing in on top U.S. AI models with Anthropic and OpenAI held back - CNBC
items.summary: The Chinese AI startup's new open source model has blown past every other open release and now sits
items.content:
The Chinese AI startup's new open source model has blown past every other open release and now sits within a percentage point of Anthropic's Opus 4.8 on one closely watched agentic benchmark, at roughly a fifth of the cost.

Developers are piling in, with OpenRouter token traffic climbing faster than it did after DeepSeek's V4 launch in April. And unlike DeepSeek, which the market eventually dismissed as a one-off chatbot shock, GLM 5.2 is strong at agentic work like planning, coding, testing, and looping, the kind of work that enterprises are racing to automate.

Companies hit by unexpectedly high AI spend on tokens, the measure of the data processed and generated by AI models, are now asking how to get the most for their money. [...] GLM 5.2 is free to download, fine-tune, and run on an enterprise's own servers, putting pricing pressure on frontier labs at the same time that access looks shaky. Anthropic had to pull its Fable Mythos-class model after an order by the Trump administration, and OpenAI announced Friday that it is limiting its GPT 5.6 models because of a government request.

The federal oversight has made a model that no one can revoke increasingly look like the safer bet.

## Read more CNBC tech news [...] Jasmine Wu@jaswu\_

WATCH LIVE

Key Points

 Zhipu's GLM 5.2 lands within a percentage point of Anthropic's Opus 4.8 on a key agentic benchmark at roughly a fifth of the cost.
 As frontier token spend strains budgets, enterprises are asking how to get the most for their money, making intelligence per dollar the metric that matters.
 With government limits restricting the rollout of Anthropic's Fable and OpenAI's GPT-5.6, a free model makes open source an attractive alternative.

In this article

 KNWAY

watch now

VIDEO52:1952:19

How Z.AI is closing in on America's AI frontier

Tech

Zhipu's GLM 5.2 artificial intelligence model landed last week with the kind of Silicon Valley buzz that followed DeepSeek's launch last year.

items.title: Yeet: The AI Dating App for Gen Z Built to Save You From Bad Chats - CNET
items.summary: Yeet's AI checks the "vibe" by analyzing photos and short phrases you provide for context clues abou
items.content:
Yeet's AI checks the "vibe" by analyzing photos and short phrases you provide for context clues about who you are. If you upload a pic of yourself at a football game, the blurb might mention the "stadium lights and school spirit."

Yeet's AI also provided feedback about the overall impression my uploads gave, including my profile pic, which Yeet softly critiqued as not yet having "peak main-photo energy."

Whatever it sees, the app will read into the mood and carry those details into your profile and into the chats you have with other users.

"That multiparty, multicontext architecture is what we've spent over two years prototyping and iterating on, and it's where our core technical differentiation lives," said Chen. [...] Even though it's clear the AI is active in every interaction on the app, none of my chatting partners batted an eyelash at Yeeta looking over their shoulders. When I asked another of my vibe-chatting partners how he felt about the AI building his profile from information in his photos or from watching us talk, he said the results were "incredibly vague," but he thought the app was "funny" and got a kick out of the programming. No red flags or worries there.

## Openly vibing

Yeeta's comments are visible to both of you while it acts as an in-chat social director, urging you to ask more about the other person's interests. But the course of true love never did run smooth, so Yeeta made a few mistakes. [...] Yeeta AI is incorporated into every element of the app's experience. It's most useful in helping romantically inclined users avoid the dreaded "Sup" and one-sided conversations that have plagued digital love-makers since the dawn of the dating swipe.

Yeet uses AI image recognition to handle the heavy lifting of building almost all the elements of your profile, letting you skip the introspection and get straight to matches.

Rather than having to build a bio from scratch, I uploaded a few pics of myself and let Yeet write the accompanying blurbs,

BOTS MEET BOUNDARIES: NVIDIA, a technology company known for AI computing and robotics systems, has introduced NVIDIA Halos for Robotics. The company calls it the industry's first full-stack, comprehensive safety system for robotics and physical AI. [...] OPINION: On June 24, OpenAI unveiled its first custom-built inference chip, developed with Broadcom and known internally as "Jalapeño." Most Americans will never see it. Yet Beijing certainly noticed. The announcement signals that the contest between America and China has moved beyond software and chatbots into a struggle for control of the infrastructure that will shape economic, military and technological power in the 21st century.

items.summary: Alistair Barr is the author of Business Insider's Tech Memo newsletter. Sign up here. Before that, h
items.content: Alistair Barr is the author of Business Insider's Tech Memo newsletter. Sign up here. Before that, he was BI's Global Tech Editor and the Big Tech team leader at Bloomberg, following a reporting career at The Wall Street Journal, USA Today, Reuters, and MarketWatch. Alistair won a Gerald Loeb Award in 2007 for coverage of short selling and was a finalist in 2013 for scoops on the Facebook IPO. More recently, he won a 2024 San Francisco Press Club award for commentary. Got a tip? items.title: Opinion: How Higher Ed Can Combat Cognitive Debt From AI Use - govtech.com
items.summary: 1. Ask students to explain AI outputs: If a student uses AI to generate an answer, they must be able
items.content:
1. Ask students to explain AI outputs: If a student uses AI to generate an answer, they must be able to explain it without the tool.
2. Teach students to interrogate the tool: Turn AI from an answer machine into a starting point for inquiry. Students should constantly ask: Why did the AI suggest this? What assumptions is it making? Whose perspectives are missing?  
   1. A fundamental flaw with AI, specifically large language models, is the perpetuation of biases found in its training data. Students should be equipped to spot these biases and make informed judgments.
   2. It’s also important to understand that AI does not evaluate truth, fairness or relevance. [...] The institutions that succeed won’t be the ones that adopt AI the fastest — those will merely be paying a premium for early access. The institutions that succeed will be the ones that focus on how AI intersects with their values, their culture and their users. They will be the ones to teach their students to use it wisely, amplifying the enduring power of human intellect, ethics, creativity and empathy.  
  
Chris Drennen is the director of academic technology and instructional support at Oberlin College and Conservatory. [...] Higher education has a choice. If we ignore AI, we do our students a disservice in preparing for the world ahead of them. On the other hand, embracing AI blindly risks the societal increase of cognitive offloading and cognitive debt. We risk producing students who are faster but less capable, passively consuming information from tools they don’t fully understand.  
  
However, if we address it directly, highlighting our respective community’s curiosities, cautions and concerns, we can teach our students how to think with AI without giving up the ability to think for themselves. AI offers unprecedented speed and scale, but it lacks the capacity for genuine human judgment.
